text: MMA just isn't boxing. A lot of what works/looks good in boxing will get you fucked up in MMA, because you have to account for more/different variables. There are basically 6 strikes in boxing, there are at least 12 standard strikes in MMA, without even getting into grappling. All of this affects your stance, spacing, timing, and punch selection. So if you just want boxing, then watch boxing, but don't disrespect these guys by implying they don't know how to fight. This gif was a little sloppy, but this is the end of the fight, both fighters are mostly gassed. Pure boxers often look similarly terrible at the end of fights. And they were intentionally just trading blows to put on a show.
label: analysis

text: Except that it’s not. A strip isn’t a statistical category unless it results in the defender gaining control of the ball(counted as a steal) but a block is regardless of who gains control of the ball after the block. As for your football analogy, a QB fumbling the ball is still a live ball until it’s recovered or goes out of bounds; also, you can’t have an incomplete pass on a sack considering that a sack is counted with the QB still having control of the ball when he is tackled but it would be considered a hurry if a defender forces an incomplete pass. This would be counted as a block since DeRozan was in the early stages of his shooting motion (Harden is credited with a block in the box score).
label: analysis

text: There was a point last season where he had the highest defensive efficiency... The "harden doesn't play defense" meme is outdated and used by people who don't watch or are only casual NBA fans and simply don't know league statistics. It's not like he all of a sudden learned how to defend; prior to the arrival of cp3, harden was the entire offense and always played on the ball, while averaging 36+ minutes per game. His defensive lapses are most likely the fact that he was exhausted and needed to conserve his energy for offense. It's just always hilarious how the guy is the league MVP and continues to put up eye-popping numbers and shows amazing displays of both offense and defense (as shown in op clip), yet there are always haters who don't even watch basketball that repeat the same misinformation from a criticism that they heard years ago.
